projects.name: Tu Día de Blanco — Hosted Digital Wedding Platform
bullets: Built a multi-tenant SaaS platform that lets couples and wedding planners design public invitation websites and run a private planning workspace — guests, seating, budget, and an AI concierge — for each wedding.

Designed a wedding-centric domain model with role-based Memberships (Owner / Editor / Viewer) and module-level permissions, supporting both a B2C couple channel (self-serve tiers) and a B2B planner channel (multi-wedding portfolios with ownership transfer).

Engineered the public Invitation Website as a fixed catalog of toggleable Sections with interchangeable Templates and Palettes, plus invite-code guest sessions gating RSVP responses and a knowledge-base AI Concierge for guest questions.

Implemented the full stack on Next.js 16 and React 19 with Supabase (Postgres, Auth, RLS) for secure multi-tenant data, Stripe for tiered subscriptions, and Vercel for deployment — backed by ADRs and a documented domain language for long-term maintainability.

projects.name: Junsu – AI Compliance Risk Evaluation Platform
bullets: Built an AI-powered compliance evaluation platform that helps identify, classify, and measure risks linked to business activities using structured LLM reasoning and calculated risk scoring.

Designed a compliance analysis application that maps organizational activities to relevant risks using large language models, combining AI judgment with rule-based calculations to produce structured, traceable risk assessments.

Implemented the project on AWS with a compliance-focused architecture, evaluating services such as Bedrock for secure model access and AWS-native deployment to handle sensitive evaluation data within centralized infrastructure.
Delivered automated risk matching, structured AI responses, scalable backend logic, and a clearer workflow for evaluating compliance exposure across different activities.
